data.content:
Here are the MSBuild evaluation performance issues in this project:

---

### 1. Deep Nested Import Chain
`EvalHeavy.csproj` → `level1.props` → `level2.props` → `level3.props`

Each `<Import>` is resolved during the **evaluation phase** — the phase where MSBuild reads and interprets all project files *before* any targets execute. Every nested import requires MSBuild to locate, open, parse, and merge another XML file. A 3-level chain like this adds cumulative I/O and parsing overhead. In large repos this anti-pattern often grows much deeper, causing evaluation time to dominate the build.

### 2. Overly Broad Glob Pattern
```xml
<AdditionalFiles Include="**\*.*" Exclude="**\bin\**;**\obj\**" />
```
`**\*.*` recursively enumerates **every file** in the project directory tree. It only excludes `bin` and `obj`, meaning it will scan into `.git/`, `node_modules/`, and any other large directories. Glob expansion happens at evaluation time, so this can make evaluation extremely slow in repos with many files.

**Fix:** Restrict the glob to specific extensions and directories, and leverage `DefaultItemExcludes`:
```xml
<AdditionalFiles Include="**\*.cs;**\*.json" />
```

### 3. File I/O via Property Function During Evaluation
```xml
<BuildNotes Condition="Exists('build-notes.txt')">
  $([System.IO.File]::ReadAllText('build-notes.txt'))
</BuildNotes>
```
This calls `System.IO.File.ReadAllText` as a **property function** during evaluation. Property functions execute inline when MSBuild evaluates `<PropertyGroup>` elements — not during target execution. Disk I/O at evaluation time blocks the entire evaluation pipeline. This should be moved into a target so it runs only during the execution phase when actually needed:
```xml
<Target Name="ReadBuildNotes" BeforeTargets="Build">
  <ReadLinesFromFile File="build-notes.txt" Condition="Exists('build-notes.txt')">
    <Output TaskParameter="Lines" PropertyName="BuildNotes" />
  </ReadLinesFromFile>
</Target>
```

---

### Evaluation vs. Execution Phase
MSBuild processes a project in two phases:
- **Evaluation** — parses XML, resolves imports, expands globs, evaluates property functions. No targets run yet. This happens on *every* build invocation and in IDE project loads.
- **Execution** — runs targets and tasks (compile, copy, etc.).

All three issues above inject unnecessary work into evaluation, slowing down every build and every IDE project reload. Moving expensive operations to targets (execution phase) and tightening globs are the highest-impact fixes.
